edges.description: A non-functional NADPH oxidase cannot generate superoxide, abolishing the respiratory burst and downstream reactive oxygen species.
edges.target: Failed respiratory burst and ROS deficiency

edges.description: Loss of oxidant-dependent killing leaves phagocytes unable to clear catalase-positive bacteria and fungi, producing recurrent severe infection.
edges.source: Failed respiratory burst and ROS deficiency
edges.target: Impaired microbial killing and recurrent infection

edges.description: Loss of ROS-dependent signaling impairs resolution of inflammation and drives exuberant granuloma formation and colitis.
edges.source: Failed respiratory burst and ROS deficiency

edges.target: Impaired microbial killing and recurrent infection
Impaired microbial killing and recurrent infection

edges.source: Autologous hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy
Avoidance of high-risk exposures (agents/circumstances to avoid)
edges.target: Impaired microbial killing and recurrent infection

nodes.description: Allogeneic HSCT is the only known cure for CGD and is associated with excellent overall and event-free survival, especially with matched donors at a younger age.

evidence.snippet:
Allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) is the only known cure 
for CGD

nodes.description: Lifelong daily antibacterial prophylaxis, standardly with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole, is recommended to prevent infections. Pregnancy caution: trimethoprim (a folic acid antagonist) is discontinued during pregnancy because of the high risk for birth defects.

evidence.snippet:
Lifelong daily antibacterial and 
antifungal prophylaxis is recommended

trimethoprim, a folic acid antagonist, is 
discontinued during pregnancy because of the high risk for birth defects

nodes.description: Lifelong daily antifungal prophylaxis, standardly with itraconazole, reduces invasive fungal infection; newer azoles (voriconazole, posaconazole, isavuconazole) have expanded therapeutic options for established fungal infection.

evidence.snippet:
Lifelong daily antibacterial and 
antifungal prophylaxis is recommended

Newer azole drugs (voriconazole, 
posaconazole, isovuconazole) have expanded therapeutic options for fungal 
infections

nodes.description: Ex vivo autologous CD34+ hematopoietic stem and progenitor cell lentiviral gene therapy (e.g., the self-inactivating G1XCGD vector for X-linked CGD) is an emerging curative approach for patients lacking a matched donor, restoring oxidase-positive neutrophils and functional superoxide production.
nodes.id: Autologous hematopoietic stem cell gene therapy

evidence.reference_title: Lentiviral gene therapy for X-linked chronic granulomatous disease.
evidence.snippet:
six of the seven surviving patients demonstrated 
stable vector copy numbers (0.4-1.8 copies per neutrophil) and the persistence 
of 16-46% oxidase-positive neutrophils

evidence.reference_title: Lentiviral gene therapy for X-linked chronic granulomatous disease.
evidence.snippet:
suggesting that autologous gene therapy is a 
promising approach for CGD patients

nodes.description: Per GeneReviews, patients with CGD should avoid decayed organic matter (mulching, gardening, leaf raking, house demolition), because inhalation of fungal spores can cause fulminant pneumonitis, and should avoid live bacterial vaccines including bacille Calmette-Guerin (BCG) and Salmonella typhi vaccination.
nodes.id: Avoidance of high-risk exposures (agents/circumstances to avoid)

evidence.snippet:
Decayed 
organic matter (e.g., mulching, gardening, leaf raking, house demolition) as 
inhalation of fungal spores can result in fulminant pneumonitis

live 
bacterial vaccines including bacille Calmette-Guérin (BCG) vaccination and 
Salmonella typhi vaccination
pathogenic variants in CYBB cause X-linked CGD

evidence.reference_title: Lentiviral gene therapy for X-linked chronic granulomatous disease.
evidence.snippet: X-CGD is caused by mutations in CYBB encoding the gp91phox subunit of the phagocyte

nodes.description: Simultaneous administration of antimicrobials and corticosteroids can help resolve the heightened inflammatory response, including colitis and obstructing granulomas.

evidence.snippet:
Simultaneous administration of antimicrobials and corticosteroids can help 
resolve the associated heightened inflammatory response, including colitis

nodes.description: Independent of infection, NADPH oxidase deficiency dysregulates innate immune signaling and impairs the resolution of inflammation, driving persistent macrophage activation, exuberant granuloma formation that can obstruct hollow viscera (gastrointestinal and genitourinary tracts), and an inflammatory-bowel-disease-like colitis.

evidence.snippet:
dysregulated inflammatory responses resulting in granuloma formation and other 
inflammatory disorders such as colitis

nodes.description: The assembled NADPH oxidase normally catalyzes the respiratory (oxidative) burst, reducing molecular oxygen to superoxide, which is dismutated and converted to hydrogen peroxide, hypohalous acids, and other reactive oxygen species within the phagosome. In CGD this burst is absent or markedly reduced, so phagocytes ingest microbes but fail to generate the oxidants required for efficient microbicidal activity.
nodes.id: Failed respiratory burst and ROS deficiency

evidence.snippet: resulting from impaired killing of bacteria and fungi

nodes.description: Because oxidant-dependent killing is lost, phagocytes cannot efficiently clear catalase-positive organisms (which detoxify their own hydrogen peroxide) such as Staphylococcus aureus, Serratia marcescens, Burkholderia cepacia, Nocardia, and Aspergillus, producing the characteristic recurrent and severe pneumonias, abscesses, lymphadenitis, and osteomyelitis.
nodes.id: Impaired microbial killing and recurrent infection

evidence.snippet: characterized by severe recurrent bacterial and fungal infections

nodes.description: Immunomodulatory prophylaxis with interferon gamma (IFN-gamma) is part of the standard prophylactic regimen in many centers.

evidence.snippet:
immunomodulatory therapy with interferon 
gamma (IFN-gamma) is part of the prophylactic regimen in many centers

nodes.description: Pathogenic variants in any one of the NOX2 complex subunits (membrane gp91phox/CYBB and p22phox/CYBA forming flavocytochrome b558; cytosolic p47phox/NCF1, p67phox/NCF2, and p40phox/NCF4; and the CYBC1/EROS chaperone that stabilizes gp91phox) prevent assembly of a functional phagocyte NADPH oxidase on the phagosomal and plasma membranes. Without a functional oxidase, electrons cannot be transferred from cytosolic NADPH across the membrane to molecular oxygen.

evidence.snippet:
identification of pathogenic variant(s) in one of six 
genes that encode or permit assembly of the subunits of phagocyte NADPH oxidase

biallelic pathogenic variants in CYBA, CYBC1, NCF1, NCF2, and NCF4 cause 
autosomal recessive CGD
